title: Diagram Viewer v1 Rebuild
content:
Rebuild the v0 D2 Diagram Viewer as `<diagram-viewer>` — a multi-file Web Component, bundled with `go tool esbuild`, served by Caddy, persisted in localStorage with JSON import/export, and verified by a full Playwright-go end-to-end suite — mirroring the story-mapper sibling project structure.

## Decomposition

Parent custom element `<diagram-viewer>` plus three sub-components:

- `<diagram-canvas>` — iframe rendering, SVG/PNG dimension detection, PNG-with-SVG-overlay handling, zoom (keys + mouse wheel), iframe link interception → `slide-navigate` event
- `<diagram-nav-tree>` — sidebar header, recursive nav tree of `layers/children/steps`, step bullets, active highlighting, click → `slide-select` event, sidebar collapse + resize
- `<diagram-help-modal>` — native `<dialog>` with keyboard shortcut reference

## Manifest format

Preserved verbatim from v0: `{ name?, title?, version?, generated?, description?, layers: [{ id, title, path, type, children?, steps?, overlay? }] }`. `loadData()` accepts this shape only; the README `{ images: [...] }` wrapper is out of scope for v1.

## localStorage persistence

Single versioned snapshot under storage key `diagramViewer.v1`:

- `version: 1`
- `manifest` (full v0-shape object or null when loaded by URL only)
- `basePath`
- `ui: { currentSlideId, zoomPercent, sidebarOpen, sidebarWidthPx }`

Stores both UI state and the full manifest so users can paste a manifest into the JSON dialog without serving any files, and a reload restores the exact view including the manifest.

## Public API

- `loadData(data)` — accept v0 shape, reject otherwise; preserves UI state when called over an existing snapshot
- `reset()` — clear localStorage and re-apply original source

## JSON import/export dialog

Native `<dialog>` with `<textarea>` + `Copy` / `Apply` / `Close` + inline error region. Apply does full-replace (not merge); rejects parse errors and `version !== 1`.

## Build & serve

- `go.mod` Go 1.26+ with `tool` directives for esbuild + caddy (mirrors story-mapper)
- `Taskfile.yaml`: `build`, `dev`, `test`, `serve`, `clean`
- `Caddyfile` on `:8080` with permissive CORS
- esbuild bundles `src/diagram-viewer.js` → `dist/diagram-viewer.js` and `src/styles.css` → `dist/diagram-viewer.css`
- `index.html` at root with `<diagram-viewer>` + inline module script that fetches `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json` and calls `loadData(data)`

## No-regression requirement

All v0 behaviour must survive the decomposition: keyboard nav (Up/Down/Left/Right/Space/Home/End/=/-/0/f/?/Esc), URL-hash sync, attribute-driven initial state (`manifest`, `base-path`, `sidebar`, `zoom`, `start-at`), sidebar resize handle, fullscreen toggle, error states for missing/invalid manifest, iframe link interception, SVG-overlay-on-PNG rendering.

## Tests

One Playwright-go test file per component, all sharing a single `TestMain` and helper set in `tests/main_test.go`:

- `tests/diagram_viewer_test.go` — parent: loadData accept/reject, JSON dialog export/import/error, Reset, persistence-across-reload
- `tests/diagram_canvas_test.go` — canvas: zoom keys, mouse-wheel zoom, SVG dimensions, PNG-with-overlay, iframe link interception, ArrowLeft history-back
- `tests/diagram_nav_tree_test.go` — sidebar: nav click, expand/collapse, sidebar collapse/toggle, resize-and-persist, keyboard nav
- `tests/diagram_help_modal_test.go` — help: ? open, Esc close, close button, full shortcut list rendered

Each test file targets the user-visible surface of its component, exercised through the real DOM.

## References

- v0: `/Users/metafeather/Developer/src/metafeather.net/codelabs/d2/viewer/`
- Sibling project mirrored: `/Users/metafeather/Developer/src/metafeather.net/metafeather-org/story-mapper/`
- Plan: `TODO.md` at project root

title: Diagram Viewer v1 — post-rebuild bug fixes
content: Independent workflow for the post-rebuild bug-fix iteration of <diagram-viewer>. Sibling to s-9gls (the original v1 rebuild). Six user-reported bugs after the v1 rebuild landed: nested-heading highlight bleeds to the parent in the sidebar, sticky :focus blue outline after click + keyboard nav, PNG/SVG-overlay misalignment on cloud-controller-manager, JSON dialog centred to viewport instead of host, JSON button in wrong toolbar (top instead of sidebar footer), and Reset button having no visible effect. Plus: move both JSON and Reset to the sidebar footer and remove the top toolbar entirely. See TODO.md at project root for the task breakdown. Implementation tracked under a parent epic with one child issue per bug, plus a leaf tests issue providing summary feedback on close.

title: Diagram Viewer v1 — bug-fix regressions (Bug 2 & 3 rework)
content: Post-implementation regression workflow for two bugs whose original fixes (under spec s-448f) did not fully resolve the user-visible issue. Bug 2 (sticky :focus blue outline): the original fix added :focus-visible CSS but never blurred the clicked link, so any subsequent arrow-key press promotes the still-focused link to :focus-visible and the blue outline returns on the previously-clicked heading. Bug 3 (PNG/SVG-overlay misalignment on cloud-controller-manager): the original fix injected zero-margin styles via iframeDoc.head — but Chrome does NOT wrap a directly-loaded SVG in HTML, so iframeDoc.head is null, the property access throws, the outer try/catch in #handleIframeLoad falls through to setDefaultDimensions(), and the iframe is sized to 100% × 100% of the canvas, leaving the SVG content rendered tiny at top-left while the PNG contain-fits to the much-bigger iframe. See TODO.md (Discovered Tasks section) for the three-task breakdown. Sibling to s-448f and s-9gls.

title: Diagram Viewer multi-instance encapsulation
content:
Allow more than one <diagram-viewer> on the same page, each loadable, navigable, zoomable, and resettable independently — with no shared localStorage state, no URL-hash collisions, no document-level keyboard cross-talk, and no leaked styles or globals.

## Failing → Passing criterion

A new examples/multi.html page with two viewers and a new Playwright suite that exercises independence. Pre-fix the suite must FAIL on shared localStorage, hash collisions, keyboard cross-talk, and double-define safety. Post-fix every test passes.

## Scope

1. Per-instance identity (#instanceId): id attr → FNV-1a hash of (manifest, base-path) → crypto.randomUUID().
2. Namespaced localStorage (diagramViewer.v1:${instanceId}); reset() only removes own key; one-time legacy migration when single-instance.
3. Opt-in URL hash via 'bookmarkable' attribute; 'primary' breaks ties when multiple bookmarkable viewers exist.
4. Scope keyboard to :focus-within (replace mouseenter/mouseleave + document keydown).
5. Eliminate residual global escapes — globalThis.focus(), persistent document mousemove/mouseup, and unguarded customElements.define calls.
6. Multi-instance demo: examples/multi.html.
7. Multi-instance Playwright tests proving independence (red→green criterion).

## Workflow

Implementation is tracked by epic i-9tby (which 'implements' this spec). The 7 tasks form a linear blocks chain so 'sudocode ready' surfaces exactly one task at a time:

  i-20ih → i-139y → i-5zjh → i-6r8b → i-7is1 → i-6xqh → i-2ess → i-9tby

At any moment exactly one issue should be ready; closing it makes the next ready, until the leaf (i-2ess) closes.

## Closure protocol

1. Tasks 1–6 (i-20ih … i-6xqh) close in chain order as their work completes.
2. When the leaf i-2ess (Playwright tests) closes:
   - the feedback anchor at line 3 ('Failing → Passing criterion') fires summary feedback onto this spec;
   - i-9tby (epic) becomes ready (no remaining blockers).
3. Close i-9tby once the feedback on this spec is reviewed and the failing→passing criterion is met.
4. Close this spec (s-7ubt) once i-9tby is closed and the feedback has been resolved or dismissed (sudocode feedback dismiss).

## Notes

- bookmarkable is OFF by default — documented breaking change for v1 single-instance users.
- Two viewers with identical (manifest, base-path) and no id share storage by design; users wanting independence give each an id.
- customElements.define is guarded across all four component files: re-loading the bundle is a no-op; conflicting constructors log a single console.warn rather than throwing.
- Shadow DOM + adoptedStyleSheets already isolate CSS from the page; the CSS-leak test is a regression guard, not a fix.

title: Diagram Viewer keyboard regression — restore keyboard after sidebar click
content:
Fix the regression where <diagram-viewer> stops responding to keyboard shortcuts after the user clicks a slide link in the sidebar. Keep the visual fix from issue i-a68b (no persistent :focus-visible blue outline on the clicked nav-item) without sacrificing keyboard navigation.

## Failing → Passing criterion

A new Playwright test loads index.html (single instance), clicks 'etcd' in the sidebar, presses ArrowDown, and asserts the active slide advanced. Pre-fix the test FAILS — link.blur() returns focus to <body>, the host's keydown listener never fires. Post-fix it PASSES — focus moves to the host element, the listener fires, and the clicked nav item still shows no :focus-visible outline.

## Root cause (verified)

src/diagram-nav-tree.js:467 calls link.blur() after dispatching slide-select. Added in i-a68b to prevent a :focus-visible blue outline appearing on the clicked link when the user subsequently presses an arrow key (browsers promote mouse-:focus → :focus-visible on the next keypress). The blur returns focus to <body> — outside the viewer's host element — so the host-scoped capture-phase keydown listener added in spec s-7ubt Task 4 never fires for any subsequent key.

The right fix is to MOVE focus to the <diagram-viewer> host (already tabindex='-1' with no :focus-visible outline), not REMOVE focus.

## Scope

1. Replace link.blur() with host-focus shift via the slide-select handler in diagram-viewer.js.
2. Regression tests: keyboard-after-sidebar-click for single and multi-instance; keep i-a68b visual guard.

## Out of scope

- Auto-focus on page load. Keyboard activation requires an in-viewer click first (single- and multi-instance).
- Non-link sidebar buttons (zoom +/−/⊡, JSON, Reset, help ?). They already keep focus on themselves and already keep keyboard alive — no change needed.

## Workflow

Implementation tracked by epic i-XXX (which 'implements' this spec). Two tasks form a linear blocks chain so 'sudocode ready' surfaces one task at a time.

## Closure protocol

1. Task 1 (impl) closes when the code change is merged and verified manually.
2. Leaf Task 2 (tests) closes after 'task test' is green; feedback anchor at the 'Failing → Passing criterion' section fires summary feedback to this spec.
3. Close the epic once both tasks are closed and the failing→passing criterion is met.
4. Close this spec once the epic closes and feedback is reviewed.

## Notes

- Iframe content clicks already focus the iframe document; existing iframe-keydown re-dispatch handler in <diagram-canvas> forwards keys — unchanged.
- :host(:focus-within) outline unchanged. Focus on the host itself shows no outline, which is the desired silent re-armament behaviour.
- Follows the s-448f → s-8sat precedent of 'regression discovered after parent spec closed'.

title: Add <diagram-loader> sibling webcomponent
content:
Introduce a new `<diagram-loader>` webcomponent that lives as a sibling of `<diagram-viewer>`, owns a manifest-path input, and hosts the JSON + Reset buttons (which are removed from the sidebar footer of `<diagram-nav-tree>`).

Targets its viewer via a `for` attribute whose value is a CSS selector (`for="#id-2"`); resolved with `document.querySelector(value)`. UI is a text input accepting a full URL or a path resolved relative to the current page, plus Load + JSON + Reset buttons. Load is a complete reload and replacement: clears that viewers localStorage snapshot, derives `base-path` from the manifest URL (strip trailing filename), fetches, calls `loadData()`. No UI state preserved across a load.

This work reverses the placement decision from closed issue `i-8g8k` which moved JSON/Reset into the sidebar footer. References [[i-8g8k]]{ discovered-from }.

Plan recorded in TODO.md.

title: Fix sticky manifest-error UI on recovery in <diagram-viewer>
content:
When `<diagram-viewer>` shows the "Failed to load manifest" overlay (after a bad path), a subsequent successful `loadFromUrl()` (or `loadData()`) call must clear the error, show the canvas, and reopen the sidebar — currently the error stays painted, the canvas remains `display:none`, and the sidebar stays collapsed.

Root cause in `src/diagram-viewer.js`:
- `#showManifestError()` appends a `<div class="error">` to `#container` with no de-duplication, sets `#canvas.style.display = "none"`, and adds `sidebar-collapsed`.
- Neither `loadFromUrl()` nor `loadData()` undoes those mutations on a successful subsequent load.
- `loadFromUrl()` clears localStorage before fetching, so `loadData()` sidebar-restore branch (which only fires with a preserved UI snapshot) is skipped on recovery.

Fix: add `#clearManifestError()` private helper that removes any `.error` direct children of `#container`, restores `#canvas.style.display`, and removes `sidebar-collapsed`. Call it at the top of `loadData()` (single recovery checkpoint) and at the top of `#showManifestError()` (idempotency for repeated failures).

Discovered after [[s-5flv]]{ discovered-from } shipped — the loader makes recovery a first-class flow which exposed a latent bug.

Plan recorded in TODO.md.

title: Single-file ESM bundle for diagram-viewer
content:
Bundle the diagram-viewer into a single ESM `dist/diagram-viewer.js` (no separate CSS file) using the exact pattern from sibling `story-mapper`: each component's shadow CSS lives in a `.css` file, imported as text via esbuild `--loader:.css=text`, wrapped once per component in a module-scoped lazy shared `CSSStyleSheet`, and adopted into each instance's shadow root via `adoptedStyleSheets`. Page-level styles (current `src/styles.css`) move out of the library and into the example pages where they belong.

Consumers can then load the viewer with one `<script type="module" src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/metafeather-org/diagram-viewer@<tag>/dist/diagram-viewer.js"></script>` tag.

## Requirements

R1. Each of the five components (`diagram-canvas`, `diagram-help-modal`, `diagram-loader`, `diagram-nav-tree`, `diagram-viewer`) has its shadow CSS in a sibling `.css` file imported as text.
R2. Each component uses a module-scoped lazy `getSharedSheet()` helper (story-mapper pattern) so all instances of the same component share one `CSSStyleSheet` object.
R3. The library no longer ships any page-level CSS; `src/styles.css` is deleted and its rules move into `index.html` and `examples/multi.html` as inline `<style>` blocks.
R4. `task build` produces only `dist/diagram-viewer.js` — no `dist/diagram-viewer.css`.
R5. Single esbuild command in `Taskfile.yaml` for both `build` and `dev`, using `--loader:.css=text`.
R6. `task test` (Playwright) passes; both single- and multi-instance pages render identically to before.
R7. `README.md` documents single-script CDN usage via `cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/...` and notes consumers own page-level layout.

## Out of scope

- Publishing to npm / adding `package.json`.
- Versioning / release tooling.

## References

- Sibling pattern: `../story-mapper/src/story-map.js` lines 8-21 (CSS import + lazy shared sheet) and `../story-mapper/Taskfile.yaml` (build flags).

title: Harden slide-path resolution via URL constructor
content:
Resolve slide paths through the browsers URL parser instead of string concatenation in <diagram-viewer>, eliminating double-encoding (%2520) when served by static site generators (e.g. Hugo) that produce non-trivial document.baseURI values.

## Problem

Currently src/diagram-viewer.js builds slide URLs with string concatenation: `${this.#basePath}/${item.path}`. The result is assigned to iframe.src. When served from environments where document.baseURI is already percent-encoded (Hugo with spaces in routes, Caddy in some configs, etc.), the browsers relative-URL resolution can produce double-encoded sequences like %2520 instead of %20.

User confirmed: SVG-internal links (resolved against the iframes own URL) work correctly, but sidebar-driven navigation (which sets iframe.src from a parent-document-relative string) double-encodes paths containing spaces.

## Decisions

- **Manifest schema**: paths must be raw/unencoded (e.g. "Control Plane/x.svg"). %xx sequences in path/overlay fields are an error, not silently normalised. Rationale: catches author mistakes early; we control the schema.
- **Stored slide.path shape**: change from relative concatenated string to fully-qualified absolute URL string (output of `new URL(rel, base).href`). Rationale: deterministic resolution regardless of host environment; simplifies URL→slide reverse matching in diagram-canvas.
- **Resolution mechanism**: single helper that wraps `new URL(relativePath, resolvedBase)`, where resolvedBase is computed once per loadData() and always ends with "/". Empty base-path falls back to document.baseURI.

## Requirements

R1. Manifest paths containing %xx sequences must throw during loadData() with a message naming the offending field and item id.
R2. Slide paths must be constructed via `new URL(rel, base).href` not string concatenation. Helper centralised on diagram-viewer.
R3. base-path with or without trailing slash must resolve identically.
R4. Empty base-path must fall back to document.baseURI.
R5. diagram-canvas URL→slide matching must work against absolute-URL slide.path values without re-deriving relative paths.
R6. A Playwright test must prove that clicking a sidebar entry for a slide whose path contains a space produces a network request URL with exactly one %20 and zero %2520.
R7. Bookmarkable hash round-trip must continue working with the new absolute-URL slide.path shape.
R8. README must document the manifest path rules (raw, unencoded) and the rationale.

## Breaking change

slide.path in the slide-change event detail changes from a relative path to an absolute URL string. External consumers parsing slide.path as relative will need to update.

## Out of scope

- Tolerating pre-encoded manifest paths (rejected — schema strictness preferred).
- Reworking how diagram-loader resolves manifest URLs (already uses `new URL(...)` correctly).

title: Always re-fetch manifest.json; merge only UI state from localStorage
content: Stop restoring stale manifest data from localStorage. Always re-fetch manifest.json when a manifest attribute is set; merge only UI state (zoom, sidebar, current slide id) from storage; discard conflicting state (e.g. removed slide ids); never silently fall back to a cached manifest on fetch errors. Implementation feedback from child issues should be summarised back on this spec on completion.

title: d2 → manifest.json generator (cmd/manifest)
content:
Build a `cmd/manifest` Go utility that reads a root `.d2` file (and any imported sub-files), walks the resolved board hierarchy via `d2lib.Compile`, and emits a `manifest.json` matching the schema in `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json`. When `--out` is given, also shell out to the `d2` binary to render the SVG tree alongside; when omitted, JSON is written to stdout and no rendering happens.

## Scope (v1)

- CLI: `--in <path>` (required), `--out <dir>` (optional), `--format <d2>` (default), trailing `--` passthrough forwarded verbatim to the underlying renderer.
- Format dispatcher with a `Format { Build, Render }` interface; only `d2` registered.
- d2 graph walk produces `Node{ID, Title, Path, Type, Overlay?, Children?, Steps?}` mirroring the existing manifest schema.
- `id` slugified from board name; `title` verbatim.
- Top-level node id hard-coded to `"overview"` matching the existing example.
- Overlay detection via sibling `*.overlay.d2` files; rendered to `*.overlay.svg` with the same passthrough flags.
- Render shells out to the `d2` binary (`exec.LookPath("d2")`); fails with install hint if missing.
- Stdout-only mode warns on stderr if `--` args are given but unused.
- Golden-file integration test against `examples/kubernetes.d2/`.
- Documentation in README.

## Out of Scope (v1)

- Top-level metadata fields (`name`, `title`, `version`, `generated`, `description`) — users decorate manually.
- Other diagramming formats (Mermaid, PlantUML, Graphviz). The `Format` interface is the seam for future support.
- d2 rendering knobs as first-class flags — handled via `--` passthrough.
- Tala (closed-source layout engine) — users pass `-- --layout tala` themselves.

## Acceptance

- `go tool manifest --in examples/kubernetes.d2/index.d2` writes JSON to stdout that deep-equals the committed golden file.
- `go tool manifest --in examples/kubernetes.d2/index.d2 --out tmp/` produces an SVG tree plus `tmp/manifest.json` whose paths all resolve to real files on disk.
- `-- --theme 200 --sketch` style passthrough is forwarded to the `d2` binary verbatim.
- Sibling `*.overlay.d2` files are detected and rendered; their nodes get `overlay: "<path>.overlay.svg"`.
- Missing `d2` binary fails fast with a clear install hint.

## Notes

- Path-sanitization risk: d2 CLI directory naming (case, spaces) is the one place graph-derived paths and on-disk paths can drift. End-to-end diff against committed `examples/kubernetes/` is the catch-net.
- Why shell out: keeps tool small, lets users pick their installed d2 version, aligns with user decision "rendering options can be handled by the d2 binary".
- Why `--` passthrough: forwards d2 flag surface verbatim without us tracking it.

Implementation summary feedback expected on this spec when the parent epic issue closes.

title: Fit SVGs to viewport along shortest side using viewBox-aware measurement
content:
Fix `<diagram-canvas>` so SVGs (especially viewBox-only ones from D2) are measured by intrinsic content size and rendered at fit-along-shortest-side ("cover") on initial load, with zoom controls and viewer resizes honouring the corrected fit.

## Background

D2 emits `<svg viewBox="0 0 W H" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">` with no `width`/`height` attributes. The current code in `src/diagram-canvas.js#handleSvgDimensions` falls through to `getBoundingClientRect()`, which returns the iframe viewport (e.g. 1000×800), not the intrinsic content size. With `preserveAspectRatio` letterboxing the content inside that rect, the measured size has no relation to the real diagram size — the eventual scaled iframe is mostly empty bands and the diagram looks small/zoomed out.

## Requirements

R1. **Spec-correct SVG dimension detection.** Replace the current `parseInt(getAttribute) ?? getBoundingClientRect()` with a helper that follows SVG2 / CSS intrinsic-sizing order:
  1. Numeric `width` / `height` attrs (both must be concrete, no `%`).
  2. `svg.viewBox.baseVal` when width > 0 && height > 0.
  3. `svg.getBBox()` (try/catch).
  4. `svg.getBoundingClientRect()` last resort.
  5. Hard `800×600` fallback only if all yield 0/NaN.
Use `parseFloat` (not `parseInt`); never coerce `"100%"` → `100`.

R2. **Fit along shortest side (cover).** Change `fitScale` in `#setDimensionsAndScale` from `Math.min(scaleX, scaleY)` to `Math.max(scaleX, scaleY)`. SVG fills viewport along its shortest dimension; longer dimension overflows scrollably (existing scroll container already supports this).

R3. **Default zoom 1.0.** Change the `1.5` default (both class field at line 47 and the first-load assignment at line 351) to `1.0` so initial load is exactly fit-scale. Update the existing test asserting `"150%"` to `"100%"` and any docs mentioning 1.5/150%.

R4. **Recompute fit on viewer resize.** Cache intrinsic dimensions on the canvas instance; attach a `ResizeObserver` in `connectedCallback`; recompute fit-scale + reapply zoom on fire (rAF-debounced, with feedback-loop guard). Disconnect in `disconnectedCallback`.

R5. **Test coverage.** Add Playwright tests with viewBox-only and percentage-width fixtures asserting `dataset.baseWidth/Height` match viewBox (not iframe rect) and `transform: scale(...)` matches the cover-fit formula at zoom 1.0. Add a resize test asserting transform updates after viewport resize.

R6. **Raster image path unchanged.** `#handleImageDimensions` already passes `naturalWidth`/`naturalHeight`, so cover-fit and resize fixes apply automatically. Verify via smoke test only.

## Out of scope

- Configurable contain/cover toggle attribute (future enhancement).
- Fit recomputation on internal iframe navigation (already covered by load handler).

## Risks / notes

- Persisted zoom in localStorage: stored `1.5` will now mean genuine 150% overflow rather than letterbox-compensated "looks fine". Acceptable; users can press `0` to reset.
- `Math.max` cover-fit means longer dimension always overflows. The viewer already provides scroll, so UX is consistent.

See TODO.md in the repo root for the implementation breakdown.
